pearl: The diagnosis of  methamphetamine use disorder (MeUD) can be made using DSM-5 criteria. pearl: DANC is not pathologic.  Most patients with Duffy-null–associated neutrophil count have no increased infection risk and do

episode_title: #491: Benign Hematology with Brad Beeler

evidence_links.rationale: Ferreira 2022 explains Duffy-null as a fixed, protective, physiologic variant supporting non-pathologic framing.

pearl: Apixaban is preferred over rivaroxaban in patients with impaired renal function (CrCl <50 mL/min), especially <30 mL/min, due to better safety data.
episode_title: 490: Update! Anticoagulation for Venous Thromboembolism with Dr. Tara Lech, PharmD

evidence_links.rationale: Lau et al. directly supports apixaban over rivaroxaban in renal impairment, especially CrCl <30.
COBRA trial showed fewer bleeding events with apixaban vs rivaroxaban in acute VTE.
Cohen supports apixaban use without dose adjustment despite renal impairment.
Buller supports DOAC safety and no apixaban dose adjustment in CKD.
Yao compares renal clearance across DOACs, explaining apixaban selection in renal impairment.

pearl: Direct oral anticoagulants (DOACs) should be avoided in patients taking strong enzyme inducers or inhibitors like carbamazepine, rifampin, or ketoconazole; these patients require warfarin.
episode_title: 490: Update! Anticoagulation for Venous Thromboembolism with Dr. Tara Lech, PharmD

evidence_links.rationale: Wiggins directly supports avoiding DOACs with strong enzyme inducers/inhibitors and bridging to warfarin.

pearl: A 3-month course of anticoagulation is sufficient for venous thromboembolism (VTE) provoked by a transient risk factor (e.g., surgery, estrogen therapy); however, patients remain at increased risk of recurrence.
episode_title: 490: Update! Anticoagulation for Venous Thromboembolism with Dr. Tara Lech, PharmD

pearl: Compression stockings are not recommended for VTE prevention during air travel in low-risk individuals, but may benefit high-risk patients (e.g., prior VTE, cancer); prophylactic low-dose anticoagulation for high-risk patients is best decided on a case-by-case basis.
episode_title: 490: Update! Anticoagulation for Venous Thromboembolism with Dr. Tara Lech, PharmD

evidence_links.rationale: Khan supports compression stockings for high-risk travelers, not recommended for low-risk individuals.

pearl: Avoid unnecessary hypercoagulable workups after unprovoked VTE; instead, focus on age-appropriate cancer screenings unless there’s a clear indication for the additional testing.
episode_title: 490: Update! Anticoagulation for Venous Thromboembolism with Dr. Tara Lech, PharmD

evidence_links.rationale: Connors supports thrombophilia rarely changing decisions, avoiding routine hypercoagulable workups.

pearl: If treatment failure is suspected, check a Xa level or D-dimer to assess for adherence or clot extension, and consider switching to low molecular weight heparin (LMWH) if failure is confirmed.
episode_title: 490: Update! Anticoagulation for Venous Thromboembolism with Dr. Tara Lech, PharmD

evidence_links.rationale: Rodger supports escalation to therapeutic LMWH when treatment failure confirmed.

pearl: Colchicine is a great option for acute gout flare, but will only work if onset of symptoms has been within 36 hours.
episode_title: #489 Live from SHM #Converge2025 Rheumatology for the Hospitalist

evidence_links.rationale: FitzGerald 2020 gout guideline cited for colchicine timing and dosing.

pearl: Preset steroid tapers (i.e. solu-medrol dose packs)  are not a good option because the steroid should not be tapered until response to therapy has been achieved. If started on a preset pack they can have rebound flare/symptoms.
episode_title: #489 Live from SHM #Converge2025 Rheumatology for the Hospitalist

pearl: Allopurinol hypersensitivity syndrome can present in a variety of rashes as well as be accompanied by systemic symptoms such as fevers, elevated eosinophils, and kidney dysfunction, so it is important to alert patients to monitor for cutaneous symptoms when starting allopurinol.
episode_title: #489 Live from SHM #Converge2025 Rheumatology for the Hospitalist

evidence_links.rationale: Yaseen 2023 cited on allopurinol hypersensitivity syndrome with cutaneous/systemic reactions.

pearl: In order to prevent gout flares, the uric acid level needs to be < 6 mg/dL, but often reference ranges will have a normal range through 8-8.5 mg/dL.
episode_title: #489 Live from SHM #Converge2025 Rheumatology for the Hospitalist

evidence_links.rationale: FitzGerald 2020 guideline targets serum uric acid goal of <6 mg/dL.

pearl: Post infectious joint or muscle pains/weakness can persist up to 6 weeks after acute infection.
episode_title: #489 Live from SHM #Converge2025 Rheumatology for the Hospitalist

pearl: Opioid use disorder and chronic pain commonly co-occur.

pearl: Reciprocal Model of Pain and Addiction: A model that describes how pain can drive substance use for short-term relief, but over time, substance use can worsen pain, creating a reinforcing cycle.

pearl: Utilize the PEG scale (pain intensity, enjoyment of life, general activity) to track pain over time.

pearl: When setting goals with patients with chronic pain, consider SMART goals –  make them specific, measurable, achievable, relevant, and time-bound.

pearl: Be aware that chronic opioid therapy for non-cancer pain has limited evidence of sustained benefit and potential for harm.

pearl: For acute flares of chronic pain, first rule out other medical causes. After appropriate work-up, Dr. Merlin recommends non-opioid management, as flares are often expected in chronic pain.

pearl: The definition of infertility is not just the traditional one of 12 or more months of unprotected heterosexual intercourse without achieving pregnancy in women under age 35 or for 6 or more months in a woman age 35 or older.  PCPs should be aware that patients who have underlying medical conditions that impact fertility (PCOS, endometriosis, sexual dysfunction) should not be held to these timelines and should get sooner evaluation.  And patients who require donor gametes/embryos also qualify as having infertility sooner.
episode_title: #486 Infertility in Clinical Practice: A Primary Care Approach With Dr Brindha Bavan

pearl: PCPs can help our patients and our colleagues in reproductive endocrinology and infertility (REI) by talking early with patients about their family planning goals and beginning basic workups
episode_title: #486 Infertility in Clinical Practice: A Primary Care Approach With Dr Brindha Bavan

pearl: Order male infertility evaluations (semen analysis, if everything is within the normal range, you’re ok) and female infertility evaluation (AMH, day 3 FSH/estradiol, hysterosalpingogram) concurrently to assess for cause of infertility.
episode_title: #486 Infertility in Clinical Practice: A Primary Care Approach With Dr Brindha Bavan

evidence_links.rationale: Semen analysis morphology criteria directly informs male infertility evaluation.
Sperm count thresholds guide interpretation of semen analysis in male evaluation.
AMH interpretation relevant to female infertility evaluation ordering.
Reference for standard female evaluation including HSG and follicle count.
Concerns HSG premedication, part of female evaluation procedure.

pearl: The infertility journey can be a financially and emotionally challenging time for our patients- be there to support their mental health as PCP and be aware that many labs may be covered by insurance if you order them with non-fertility related codes but may need to be paid out of pocket if the fertility specialist orders them (depending on insurance coverage)
episode_title: #486 Infertility in Clinical Practice: A Primary Care Approach With Dr Brindha Bavan

pearl: Over one-third of U.S. adults have prediabetes, the vast majority of whom don’t know they have it; thus, screening is essential.
episode_title: #481 Prediabetes and Diabetes Prevention with Dr. Scott Isaacs

evidence_links.rationale: USPSTF screening recommendation supports the need to screen for prediabetes.

pearl: The three methods of screening for prediabetes are: Hemoglobin A1c, fasting plasma glucose, and 2-hour plasma glucose during an oral glucose tolerance test (OGTT). The OGTT can be a helpful option in situations in which the validity of the A1c is in question (for instance, in conditions that impact RBC turnover) and the fasting plasma glucose is borderline.

supporting_citations.citation_label: American Diabetes Association Professional Practice Committee, 2024

episode_title: #481 Prediabetes and Diabetes Prevention with Dr. Scott Isaacs

evidence_links.rationale: Warren et al supports A1c as preferred single test correlating with outcomes.
Radin supports using OGTT when A1c validity is questionable due to altered RBC turnover.
Kim et al supports A1c pitfall with increased RBC turnover falsely lowering A1c.
Abdul-Ghani explains physiologic differences of IFG vs IGT.

pearl: Dr. Isaacs emphasizes a complications-centric rather than glucocentric approach when discussing prediabetes with patients, centering his conversations around big-picture  mitigation of overall cardiometabolic risk / multi-morbidities rather than just focusing on the blood sugar.
episode_title: #481 Prediabetes and Diabetes Prevention with Dr. Scott Isaacs

evidence_links.rationale: Samson et al is cited for complications-centric approach and AACE goals.

pearl: It is important to  keep an eye out for atypical cases of prediabetes, including pre-type 1 diabetes, pancreatic prediabetes, and prediabetes associated with endocrinopathies (Cushing’s and acromegaly).
episode_title: #481 Prediabetes and Diabetes Prevention with Dr. Scott Isaacs

evidence_links.citation_label: American Diabetes Association Professional Practice Committee, 2024
evidence_links.rationale: ADA guidance on pre-type 1 diabetes screening supports atypical case awareness.
Hart et al supports pancreatic cancer/prediabetes link (pancreatic prediabetes).
Rickels distinguishes pancreatic diabetes as atypical form.
Pelsma supports cortisol-related dysglycemia (endocrinopathy).
Prete supports mild autonomous cortisol secretion raising dysglycemia risk.
Fleseriu supports acromegaly diagnosis, an endocrinopathy cause.
Nederstigt supports screening pre-type 1 with autoimmune disease.
Notkins on GAD-65 antibodies for type 1 diabetes screening.

pearl: Intensive lifestyle changes resulting in a 7% weight loss can decrease the risk of progression from prediabetes to diabetes by 58%, as shown in the landmark Diabetes Prevention Program study. Medications like metformin, pioglitazone, and GLP-1 agonists are also important options in the toolbox for prediabetes management.
episode_title: #481 Prediabetes and Diabetes Prevention with Dr. Scott Isaacs

evidence_links.rationale: DPP found 7% weight loss reduced progression by 58% and metformin by 31%.
DPP subgroup analysis on metformin effectiveness.
ACT NOW trial supports pioglitazone for diabetes prevention.
IRIS trial supports pioglitazone preventing diabetes progression.
SURMOUNT supports tirzepatide (GLP-1/GIP) reducing diabetes risk.
Kahn supports semaglutide lowering diabetes risk.
Perreault supports semaglutide reducing diabetes risk.
SCALE trial supports liraglutide for diabetes prevention.
STOP-NIDDM shows acarbose reduces diabetes risk, part of the toolbox.

pearl: Before diagnosing depression, always rule out other medical causes (e.g., hypothyroidism, obstructive sleep apnea, anemia, vitamin deficiencies) through thorough patient history and medical workup.

pearl: Understand the side effect profiles of antidepressants to guide medication selection.

evidence_links.rationale: Edinhoff details SSRI black box warning, relevant to side effect profile.

pearl: Consider referral to a psychiatrist when the patient’s depression management becomes too challenging and they are not improving in their symptoms.

pearl: If a patient has failed two full trials of antidepressant medications (adequate dose and duration), they are considered to have treatment-resistant depression.

episode_title: #479 Managing Treatment-Resistant Depression featuring Dr. Jessi Gold

evidence_links.rationale: Gaynes defines TRD as failure of two or more complete antidepressant trials.

pearl: If a patient has a partial response to an antidepressant, consider adding an adjunct medication (e.g., bupropion, aripiprazole) rather than switching medications entirely.
episode_title: #479 Managing Treatment-Resistant Depression featuring Dr. Jessi Gold

pearl: For treatment-resistant depression, consider advanced therapies like TMS, which stimulates brain regions involved in mood regulation or ECT for severe cases with psychosis or suicidality.

pearl: Newer therapies, including ketamine and psilocybin, are also becoming more readily available. Tailoring these treatments based on individual patient needs and careful monitoring is essential for optimal management.

pearl: Go back to the basics and ask yourself, “Does this patient have heart failure?” Watch out for mimics of heart failure.

pearl: Reducing afterload can augment diuretic output in patients with volume overload.

pearl: Avoid SABA monotherapy; prioritize ICS-formoterol as the new reliever therapy given the fast onset of formoterol. It can be used as a maintenance inhaler if more control is needed. Avoid salmeterol due to delayed onset.
clinical_topic: Effectiveness of ICS-formoterol for asthma control
episode_title: #468: Asthma Update with Cyrus Askin

Beasley 2019 supports anti-inflammatory reliever over SABA monotherapy.
O'Byrne 2018 supports ICS-formoterol rapid relief over albuterol monotherapy.
NAEPPCC 2020 informs ICS-formoterol and MART dosing.

pearl: For patients with a SABA who are  unable to obtain access to ICS-formoterol, tell them to use an ICS inhaler whenever they use their SABA.

evidence_links.rationale: Papi 2022 supports adding ICS when albuterol is used.

pearl: Clinical history is important. In addition to assessing asthma triggers like dust, wildfires, and pollen, ask about a  history of premature birth (before 34 weeks) as this increases the risk of bronchopulmonary dysplasia and underdeveloped terminal airways, which is a risk factor for asthma.
episode_title: #468: Asthma Update with Cyrus Askin

pearl: Spirometry should be used to diagnose asthma, but peak expiratory flow (PEF) can be used if spirometry is unavailable.

pearl: When spirometry is normal during asymptomatic periods, use bronchoprovocation testing with methacholine to confirm airway hyperresponsiveness. This is particularly sensitive for diagnosing asthma.

episode_title: #468: Asthma Update with Cyrus Askin

evidence_links.rationale: Shin 2023 supports that normal spirometry doesn't rule out asthma, justifying methacholine.
Yurdakul supports high sensitivity of bronchoprovocation testing.

pearl: Utilize the ACT questionnaire to assess and monitor asthma control over time. Scores below 20 suggest poorly-controlled asthma and should prompt therapy adjustments.
clinical_topic: Effectiveness of ICS-formoterol for asthma control
episode_title: #468: Asthma Update with Cyrus Askin

evidence_links.rationale: Soler 2019 validates ACT for monitoring asthma control.

pearl: Refer early for severe or poorly-controlled asthma, asthma-COPD overlap, or obesity-associated asthma.
clinical_topic: Asthma phenotypes, especially T2-high and obesity-associated asthma
episode_title: #468: Asthma Update with Cyrus Askin

evidence_links.rationale: Freiler 2015 supports evaluation for asthma-COPD overlap.

evidence_links.paper_title: Global Strategy for Asthma Management and Prevention (GINA 2024 Strategy Report)
evidence_links.rationale: GINA 2024 provides referral criteria.

pearl: Use personal PEF measurements to assist in creating an asthma action plan, which can aid in diagnosing and triaging asthma exacerbations.

supporting_citations.paper_title: Global Strategy for Asthma Management and Prevention (GINA 2024 Strategy Report)

clinical_topic: Asthma action plans and peak flow-based self-management
episode_title: #468: Asthma Update with Cyrus Askin

evidence_links.rationale: Couri 2019 supports asthma action plans using personal best peak flow.

pearl: Encourage healthy weight management, regular exercise, routine vaccination, and pulmonary rehabilitation to improve asthma outcomes.
clinical_topic: Weight management as part of asthma care
episode_title: #468: Asthma Update with Cyrus Askin

evidence_links.rationale: Juel 2012 supports weight management to improve asthma control.
Vasileiou 2020 supports vaccination to prevent asthma exacerbation.

pearl: Use the Geriatrics 5Ms to manage unintentional weight loss (and other chronic diseases/geriatric syndromes).

episode_title: #467 Unintentional Weight Loss with Dr. Eva Szymanski

evidence_links.rationale: Holliday et al 2022 supports the 5Ms model, which enumerates the five domains.

pearl: Dealing with uncertainty and complexity in geriatric syndromes is challenging– know that you are not alone and this is important work!
episode_title: #467 Unintentional Weight Loss with Dr. Eva Szymanski

pearl: Ask about alcohol use, family history and metabolic comorbidities when taking a history to determine the etiology of cirrhosis.
episode_title: #466 Cirrhosis Update with Scott Matherly

pearl: An ALT >25 IU/l for females and >33 IU/l for males is abnormal
episode_title: #466 Cirrhosis Update with Scott Matherly

evidence_links.rationale: Kwo 2016 defines abnormal ALT thresholds of >25 for females and >33 for males.

pearl: FIB-4 score can be calculated to evaluate the risk of advanced fibrosis based on labs
episode_title: #466 Cirrhosis Update with Scott Matherly

evidence_links.rationale: Duarte-Rojo 2024 referenced for FIB-4 interpretation and fibrosis risk cutoffs.

pearl: Decompensation is the development of clinically evident portal hypertension including overt ascites, variceal bleeding and overt hepatic encephalopathy.
episode_title: #466 Cirrhosis Update with Scott Matherly

pearl: The median survival of patients drops significantly following decompensation from 12 years to 1.5 to 2 years.
episode_title: #466 Cirrhosis Update with Scott Matherly

evidence_links.rationale: D'Amico 2006 documents median survival drop from ~12 years to ~2 years after decompensation.
Tandon 2021 cited to support ~2 year survival in decompensated cirrhosis.

pearl: Carvedilol is the preferred beta blocker for management of esophageal varices and portal hypertension.
episode_title: #466 Cirrhosis Update with Scott Matherly

evidence_links.rationale: AASLD guideline identifies carvedilol as preferred non-selective beta-blocker for portal hypertension.
Sinagra 2014 shows carvedilol lowers portal pressures better than propranolol.
PREDESCI shows carvedilol decreases decompensation, supporting its use.

pearl: Patients with an original MELD >18 have a higher risk of mortality and liver failure following a TIPS.
episode_title: #466 Cirrhosis Update with Scott Matherly

evidence_links.rationale: Biggins 2021 directly supports MELD >18 higher mortality/liver failure after TIPS.
Ferral 2004 cited with Biggins as evidence for MELD >18 TIPS risk.

pearl: Clinically relevant portal hypertension is typically seen when the hepatic venous pressure gradient is greater than 10.
episode_title: #466 Cirrhosis Update with Scott Matherly

evidence_links.rationale: Garcia-Tsao 2017 defines HVPG thresholds including clinically significant portal hypertension >10 mmHg.

pearl: A MELD score >15 is a critical threshold where the benefits of liver transplantation outweigh the risks of waiting. Individuals with liver decompensation or MELD >15 should be referred for transplant evaluation.
episode_title: #466 Cirrhosis Update with Scott Matherly

evidence_links.rationale: Aberg 2015 supports transplant referral for decompensation or MELD >15.
Luo 2018 supports survival benefit of transplant for MELD >15.

pearl: Clinical assessment is vital: patients with a convincing history can increase or decrease the pretest probability that a patient is hypogonadal, thus influencing your interpretation of borderline values.
episode_title: #462 Male Hypogonadism Pearls with Dr. Bradley Anawalt: Insights on Testosterone Deficiency

pearl: Patients with signs and symptoms of low testosterone should be diagnosed with testosterone deficiency only when AM fasting testosterone is low on two occasions, using CDC validated laboratory methods whenever possible.
episode_title: #462 Male Hypogonadism Pearls with Dr. Bradley Anawalt: Insights on Testosterone Deficiency

evidence_links.rationale: Travison 2017 established the CDC-validated reference range for testosterone.
Jucá 2019 shows fasting affects testosterone levels, supporting AM fasting measurement.

pearl: Using accurate assays are vital to the diagnosis of hypogonadism, especially if presenting symptoms are vague and initial values are borderline.
episode_title: #462 Male Hypogonadism Pearls with Dr. Bradley Anawalt: Insights on Testosterone Deficiency

evidence_links.rationale: Travison 2017 emphasizes standardization/accuracy of testing across labs.

pearl: Free testosterone is rarely discrepant from total testosterone, but may happen in cases where sex-hormone binding globulin (SHBG) is altered such as obesity (lowers the SHBG, thus potentially showing a normal free testosterone even when total appears low) or old age (elevates the SHBG, thus possibly causing a normal total testosterone with low free testosterone).
episode_title: #462 Male Hypogonadism Pearls with Dr. Bradley Anawalt: Insights on Testosterone Deficiency

evidence_links.rationale: Antonio 2016 shows aging raises SHBG and lowers free testosterone more than total.
Gyawali 2018 shows metabolic syndrome lowers SHBG causing low total with normal free T.
Liang 2021 explains SHBG elevation affecting free vs total testosterone.

pearl: Labs that directly measure free testosterone are erroneous and should not be used. Free testosterone should be calculated (most commonly done) or measured by equilibrium dialysis (expensive, rarely done).
episode_title: #462 Male Hypogonadism Pearls with Dr. Bradley Anawalt: Insights on Testosterone Deficiency

evidence_links.rationale: Wang 2009 supports calculated free T and states direct measurements are not useful.
Jasuja 2022 validates equilibrium dialysis as accurate free T measurement.

pearl: Decreased libido is the most specific symptom of hypogonadism, especially when it is acute onset. Make sure patients are being realistic and not simply comparing themselves to when they were younger.
episode_title: #462 Male Hypogonadism Pearls with Dr. Bradley Anawalt: Insights on Testosterone Deficiency

pearl: Many symptoms of hypogonadism are nonspecific, and it is important to consider other  etiologies such as depression.
episode_title: #462 Male Hypogonadism Pearls with Dr. Bradley Anawalt: Insights on Testosterone Deficiency

pearl: A testicular exam to assess the size of the testes and exclude a mass is essential. Orchidometers are inexpensive tools that can aid in testicular size assessments.
episode_title: #462 Male Hypogonadism Pearls with Dr. Bradley Anawalt: Insights on Testosterone Deficiency

pearl: There is evidence in the literature that lifestyle modification (i.e. weight loss via diet and exercise) improves many symptoms of testosterone deficiency in men who have a high BMI, low serum testosterone, and no identifiable disorder of the hypothalamus-pituitary- testicular axis.
episode_title: #462 Male Hypogonadism Pearls with Dr. Bradley Anawalt: Insights on Testosterone Deficiency

evidence_links.rationale: Grosmann 2024 T4DM secondary analysis shows lifestyle modification improved QoL in high-BMI men.
T4DM found lifestyle modification outperformed testosterone for quality of life.
Yeap 2016 supports obesity-driven low T and prioritizing lifestyle modification.
Zhai 2016 explains obesity/leptin physiology reversed by weight loss.

pearl: Remember to use Z scores and not T scores for bone density assessment of pre-menopausal patients. Additionally, the American College of Sports Medicine considers Z scores of less than -1 to be indicative of low bone density in athletes, rather than the conventional less than -2.

pearl: Patients with tension side (lateral) femoral neck stress fractures should have emergent orthopaedic surgery consultation. Compression side (medial) femoral neck stress fractures can often be managed conservatively.

pearl: Newer studies have illustrated that trochanteric pain is rarely “bursitis,” but in fact usually tendinopathy of the surrounding structures! Consider the involvement of surrounding gluteal tendons, IT band, and tensor fasciae latae.
episode_title: #455 Sports Injuries Part 2: Hip and Knee

evidence_links.rationale: Bird 2001 supports trochanteric pain being tendinopathy rather than true bursitis.
Speers 2017 cited to support imaging-based understanding of trochanteric pain as tendinopathy.

pearl: Physical therapy and corticosteroid injections can both be effective for treatment of trochanteric pain, particularly in the short term, however, steroid injections may not be as effective in the long term.
episode_title: #455 Sports Injuries Part 2: Hip and Knee

evidence_links.rationale: Mellor 2018 shows PT primary, steroid injections helpful short term but less effective at 52 weeks.
Bisset 2006 extrapolated to concern about steroids worsening long-term tendinopathy outcomes.
Coombes 2010 cited on possible worse long-term outcomes of steroid injections for tendinopathy.
Couppe 2022 discusses uncertain role and possible adverse long-term effects of steroid injections.

pearl: Identifying patients with meniscus tears who would benefit from surgery can be complex. Factors that favor surgical intervention are acute tears versus chronic or degenerative tears, younger patients versus older, and lack of concurrent osteoarthritis of the knee.
episode_title: #455 Sports Injuries Part 2: Hip and Knee

evidence_links.rationale: Bassett 2017 shows no surgical advantage in older degenerative tears with OA, supporting factors favoring/disfavoring surgery.
Kise 2016 shows no surgery advantage in older degenerative meniscus tears with OA.
Chatain 2003 supports surgery effectiveness in young patients with acute meniscal injuries and healthy cartilage.

pearl: Patients with meniscus tears and symptoms of joint “locking” should be made non-weight-bearing and urgently referred to an Orthopedic surgeon for evaluation for surgery to treat a bucket handle tear.
episode_title: #455 Sports Injuries Part 2: Hip and Knee

pearl: Patients with severe mental illness have a 10-20 year shorter life expectancy than the general population, attributable to physical life-limiting illness including cardiovascular disease and metabolic diseases. This is referred to as the mortality gap.

evidence_links.rationale: Fiorillo directly states 10-20 year mortality gap driven by cardiovascular/metabolic disease.

pearl: Due to the mortality gap, it is important to risk-stratify patients with psychiatric illness for cardiovascular disease by checking a hemoglobin A1c and lipid panel.

evidence_links.rationale: Mortality gap from CV/metabolic disease supports risk-stratifying, though it doesn't specify A1c/lipid testing.

pearl: A cognitive assessment is important to diagnose delirium, which may mimic a psychiatric diagnosis. If short on time, focus on assessing areas of attention rather than orientation.

evidence_links.rationale: Setters shows delirium increases mortality, supporting importance of cognitive assessment.

pearl: In triaging the patient, remember that psychiatry units are designed as milieu units to encourage patients to be mobile and physically engage in care. If a patient is unable to participate due to ongoing medical illness, likely they are better served on the medical floor.
episode_title: #446 Psychiatry Primer for the Hospitalist

evidence_links.rationale: Integrated care/LOS study relates to triaging medical vs psychiatric floor placement.

pearl: Macrocytosis has two major mechanisms 1) lots of young, normal cells due to reticulocytosis (“big, juicy, young cells”) and 2) abnormal cells caused by abnormalities in lipid metabolism or  red cell membranes (e.g. liver disease, alcohol use) or  DNA synthesis with impaired cell division (e.g. B12 deficiency).
segment: 25:10 Autoimmune Hemolytic Anemia: Diagnosis and Treatment
clinical_topic: 25:10 Autoimmune Hemolytic Anemia: Diagnosis and Treatment

evidence_links.rationale: Hoffbrand supports alcohol causing macrocytosis via lipid abnormalities, DNA damage, liver disease.

pearl: The differential diagnosis (DDx) for macrocytosis includes reticulocytosis, medications (e.g., antivirals, antimetabolites, antineoplastic agents), B12/folate deficiency, liver disease, chronic alcohol use, myelodysplastic syndrome (MDS), and VEXAS!
segment: 25:10 Autoimmune Hemolytic Anemia: Diagnosis and Treatment
clinical_topic: 25:10 Autoimmune Hemolytic Anemia: Diagnosis and Treatment

evidence_links.rationale: Kaferle notes common meds (allopurinol, metformin) causing macrocytosis.
Grayson cites VEXAS as a cause of macrocytosis.
Hoffbrand supports alcohol/liver as macrocytosis cause in the DDx.

pearl: Elevated bilirubin is not sensitive for hemolytic anemia, so check LDH, haptoglobin, and a reticulocyte count (Dr. Naik prefers to send a reticulocyte count with every CBC).
episode_title: #444 Macrocytosis, Hemolytic Anemia, Myelodysplastic Syndrome

evidence_links.rationale: Khoury supports checking reticulocyte count with every CBC and using absolute count.

pearl: The DDx for hemolytic anemia includes immune mediated (autoimmune disease, hemoglobinurias, transfusion reactions), trauma (e.g., marching, cardiac valves), microangiopathic (e.g., MAHA, DIC), drugs, hemoglobinopathies (e.g., sickle cell), G6PD, hereditary spherocytosis, infections (e.g., malaria), and lymphomas (esp. CLL).
episode_title: #444 Macrocytosis, Hemolytic Anemia, Myelodysplastic Syndrome

evidence_links.rationale: Brodsky covers non-immune Coombs-negative hemolytic anemia DDx.
Barcellini cited for drug-induced hemolytic anemia in the DDx.
Hill frames the broader hemolytic anemia differential.

pearl: A positive direct antiglobulin test (DAT) confirms a diagnosis of autoimmune hemolytic anemia.
episode_title: #444 Macrocytosis, Hemolytic Anemia, Myelodysplastic Syndrome

evidence_links.rationale: Hill supports DAT in autoimmune hemolytic anemia diagnosis.
Aringer connects DAT positivity with autoimmune disease (lupus) evaluation.

pearl: Refer to Hematology for suspected MDS when patients have numerous immature cells on a CBC with differential, progressive cytopenias (trajectory) and/or younger age.

pearl: Coagulopathy of Cirrhosis w/ Dr. Christin DeStefano
episode_title: #441 More Clinical Pearls (New antibiotics, syphilis, heart disease, cirrhosis, lots more!) from ACP #IM2024

pearl: Dr. DeStefano covered a wide swath of content around cirrhosis and coagulopathy, describing that hemostasis in cirrhosis is rebalanced, with changes occurring in both procoagulant and anticoagulant factors. It can therefore be hard to tell where on the pro-bleeding to  pro-clotting spectrum these patients are.  Labs such as PT/INR, PTT, Platelet count, and fibrinogen are not reliable in determining the risk of bleeding or clotting in these patients and should not be targets for repletion in clinically stable patients (“Don’t treat a number” is the general recommendation, here).  Administration of blood products on the basis of labs can alter normal homeostasis in these patients and also cause volume shifts, worsening of portal hypertension, and lead to cirrhosis decompensation.

pearl: Updated CDC guidelines recommend that all adults should be screened for Hepatitis B at least once in their lifetime. pearl: Hepatitis B screening consists of “triple testing” for Hepatitis B surface antigen, Hepatitis B surface antibody, and Hepatitis B core antibody. pearl: Three clinical buckets that raise suspicion for APLS include patients who are clotting through anticoagulation, patients clotting in unusual locations, or patients with a history of autoimmunity. These three buckets also help inform when to perform antiphospholipid antibody laboratory testing.
episode_title: #421 Antiphospholipid Syndrome with Dr. Arielle Langer

evidence_links.rationale: Choosing Wisely emphasizes pre-test probability and avoiding testing in clear provoked clots, informing when to test.

pearl: Antiphospholipid antibody laboratory testing indicates whether antibodies are present that recognize clotting proteins, not that they actually have the special property that enables them to activate the clotting cascade. Thus, false positive testing is a challenge. In particular, transient false positives are common in the setting of acute inflammation, which is why diagnostic criteria require that testing is persistently positive for at least 12 weeks.
episode_title: #421 Antiphospholipid Syndrome with Dr. Arielle Langer

evidence_links.rationale: Revised Sapporo criteria require persistent laboratory positivity for at least 12 weeks.
Blood donor data show positive antibodies without disease, illustrating false positives.

pearl: Several trials indicate that warfarin is superior to DOACs for treatment of APLS. For patients who are experiencing breakthrough thrombosis on warfarin, some options include addition of aspirin to warfarin, switching to enoxaparin, addition of hydroxychloroquine, or addition of eculizumab.
episode_title: #421 Antiphospholipid Syndrome with Dr. Arielle Langer

evidence_links.rationale: Rivaroxaban arm stopped early for higher thrombosis than warfarin in triple-positive APLS.

Supports switching to enoxaparin for breakthrough thrombosis on warfarin.

pearl: Patients may have thrombotic APLS only, obstetric APLS only, or both, depending on whether their antiphospholipid antibodies recognize clotting proteins only, placental trophoblasts only, or both.

pearl: Ask a 24-hour recall from patients on what they are eating… without judging!
episode_title: #412 Low Carb, Keto, Fasting: Lifestyle Interventions for Weight Management

pearl: Low carb diet (typically under 20-50 grams per day) is popularly referred to as ketogenic diet. Dr. Yancy notes <20 grams carbs per day will put most patients in ketosis.
episode_title: #412 Low Carb, Keto, Fasting: Lifestyle Interventions for Weight Management

evidence_links.paper_title: Very-Low-Carbohydrate Ketogenic Diet v. Low-Fat Diet for Long-Term Weight Loss: A Meta-Analysis of Randomised Controlled Trials
evidence_links.rationale: Meta-analysis of very-low-carb ketogenic diets supports the low-carb/keto concept.

evidence_links.paper_title: An Online Intervention Comparing a Very Low-Carbohydrate Ketogenic Diet and Lifestyle Recommendations Versus a Plate Method Diet in Overweight Individuals With Type 2 Diabetes: A Randomized Controlled Trial
evidence_links.rationale: RCT of very low-carb ketogenic diet informs keto definition context.

pearl: Reduce insulin dose by half upon starting a low carbohydrate diet to prevent hypoglycemia.
episode_title: #412 Low Carb, Keto, Fasting: Lifestyle Interventions for Weight Management

pearl: Technically no carb foods (meat, poultry, fish, eggs) are unlimited but that does not mean a patient should eat them or add fats indiscriminately.  Recommend telling patients to eat to satiated and not beyond.
episode_title: #412 Low Carb, Keto, Fasting: Lifestyle Interventions for Weight Management

pearl: If drinking, lower carbohydrate alcohols are better.
episode_title: #412 Low Carb, Keto, Fasting: Lifestyle Interventions for Weight Management

pearl: Intermittent fasting has evidence to help with weight loss and metabolism.
episode_title: #412 Low Carb, Keto, Fasting: Lifestyle Interventions for Weight Management

evidence_links.rationale: Review of intermittent fasting mechanisms and metabolic health.
Review on fasting improving insulin sensitivity and metabolic health.
Review found intermittent fasting effective for reducing body fat.

pearl: The hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal (HPA) axis is responsible for regulation and production of cortisol
episode_title: #411 Adrenal Insufficiency with Dr. Atil Kargi

evidence_links.rationale: Husebye 2021 cited for background on the HPA axis.

pearl: The most common etiology of primary adrenal insufficiency (PAI) in western societies is autoimmune adrenalitis.
episode_title: #411 Adrenal Insufficiency with Dr. Atil Kargi

evidence_links.rationale: Bornstein 2016 cited for autoimmune adrenalitis as most common cause in developed countries.

pearl: After a diagnosis of PAI is made, the 21-hydroxylase antibody should be checked for autoimmune adrenalitis.
episode_title: #411 Adrenal Insufficiency with Dr. Atil Kargi

evidence_links.rationale: Bornstein 2016 referenced for diagnostic evaluation after PAI diagnosis.
Husebye 2021 supports 21-hydroxylase antibody testing after PAI diagnosis.

pearl: Typically in PAI, cortisol will be low and ACTH will be elevated.
episode_title: #411 Adrenal Insufficiency with Dr. Atil Kargi

pearl: For secondary adrenal insufficiency, cortisol will be low and ACTH will typically be low or normal.
episode_title: #411 Adrenal Insufficiency with Dr. Atil Kargi

pearl: Cortisol levels should be checked around the time a patient usually wakes up, typically from 7 to 9 am.
episode_title: #411 Adrenal Insufficiency with Dr. Atil Kargi

pearl: When ACTH >300, the adrenal glands are maximally stimulated, and no change in cortisol levels would be expected with a stimulation test. Therefore a stimulation test may be unnecessary when basal A TH levels are above 300 pg/mL.
episode_title: #411 Adrenal Insufficiency with Dr. Atil Kargi

pearl: Since cortisol is protein bound, low total cortisol with normal free cortisol can be seen with low albumin/protein states including chronic liver disease and sepsis.
episode_title: #411 Adrenal Insufficiency with Dr. Atil Kargi

pearl: Oral estrogen replacement can lead to total serum cortisol levels 50-70% higher than normal with normal free cortisol.
episode_title: #411 Adrenal Insufficiency with Dr. Atil Kargi

evidence_links.rationale: Qureshi 2007 quantifies oral estrogen effect raising cortisol 50-70%.

pearl: Ensure patients with adrenal insufficiency have a medic alert bracelet and an emergency hydrocortisone injection  kit to avoid or treat adrenal crisis.
episode_title: #411 Adrenal Insufficiency with Dr. Atil Kargi

evidence_links.rationale: Rushworth 2019 supports home hydrocortisone emergency kit for AI patients.

pearl: Consistent classification of severity of  diabetic foot infections is important for management. These include  no infection, mild infection (superficial soft tissue), moderate infection (involves deeper structure(s), and severe infection (signs of sepsis and systemic manifestations).

supporting_citations.citation_label: JVS Practice Guideline Mills, Sr., et. al, 2014

episode_title: #410 Diabetic Foot Infections

evidence_links.rationale: IWGDF 2019 is cited as the basis for classifying infections into no/mild/moderate/severe categories.

pearl: X-ray can be specific if osteomyelitis is detected, but x-ray findings can lag behind.  MRI is useful when x-ray and other available features and labs are ambiguous.
episode_title: #410 Diabetic Foot Infections

evidence_links.rationale: IDSA guideline recommends X-ray and notes findings may lag by weeks.

evidence_links.paper_title: Role of Inflammatory Markers in Diagnosing
evidence_links.rationale: Inflammatory markers help decide when MRI is needed for ambiguous osteomyelitis.

pearl: The minimum evaluation for vascular disease in a patient with diabetic foot infection is a good history and physical (e.g., history of claudication, co-morbidities, tobacco use). Ultimately, this is still not enough to exclude PAD. Ideally, studies like ankle-systolic pressures or toe pressure index would be used to evaluate all patients with diabetic foot infections.

supporting_citations.citation_label: JVS Practice Guideline Mills, Sr., et. al, 2014

episode_title: #410 Diabetic Foot Infections

evidence_links.rationale: APMA/SVM guidelines recommend non-invasive vascular testing since history/exam cannot exclude PAD.

pearl: The WIfI scoring system (wound, ischemia and foot infection) can help predict who would benefit from revascularization and who is high risk for needing amputation.

supporting_citations.citation_label: JVS Practice Guideline Mills, Sr., et. al, 2014

episode_title: #410 Diabetic Foot Infections

evidence_links.citation_label: JVS Practice Guideline Mills, Sr., et. al, 2014
evidence_links.rationale: JVS guideline describes WIfI scoring predicting revascularization benefit and amputation risk.

pearl: The results of a superficial wound swab do not correlate with the pathogen causing infection so it is best to get a tissue culture at the time of debridement.
episode_title: #410 Diabetic Foot Infections

pearl: Signs of gangrene, undrained abscess or evidence of gas on imaging should prompt anaerobic coverage.
episode_title: #410 Diabetic Foot Infections

pearl: There are four principles that promote good wound healing. They are :  removal of  necrotic tissue (i.e., debridement), appropriate antibiotics, achieving adequate blood flow, and creating a good wound healing environment (e.g., offloading, tobacco cessation, good glycemic control).
episode_title: #410 Diabetic Foot Infections

pearl: Bothersome menopausal symptoms can start in a patient’s early to mid-forties even while they still have a regular menstrual cycle.  The duration of VMS is highly variable.
episode_title: #409 Hormonal and Nonhormonal Therapy for Vasomotor Symptoms of Menopause

pearl: The diagnosis of COPD is a clinical one– you must have the appropriate history, exposure and spirometry to make the diagnosis!
episode_title: #408 COPD in Older Adults with Leah Witt, MD & Amber Lanae Martirosov, PharmD

evidence_links.paper_title: Global Strategy for the Diagnosis, Management, and Prevention of Chronic Obstructive Pulmonary Disease: 2023 Report
evidence_links.rationale: GOLD guideline defines spirometric diagnostic criteria (post-bronchodilator FEV1/FVC <0.7) for COPD.

pearl: Update your COPD alphabet: ABCD is now ABE with a focus on exacerbations.
episode_title: #408 COPD in Older Adults with Leah Witt, MD & Amber Lanae Martirosov, PharmD

evidence_links.paper_title: Global Strategy for the Diagnosis, Management, and Prevention of Chronic Obstructive Pulmonary Disease: 2023 Report
evidence_links.rationale: GOLD 2023 report reflects the ABCD to ABE classification update focusing on exacerbations.

pearl: The natural history of lung aging includes thoracic cage changes, reduced elastic recoil, and reduced respiratory muscle strength
episode_title: #408 COPD in Older Adults with Leah Witt, MD & Amber Lanae Martirosov, PharmD

evidence_links.rationale: Janssens 2005 discusses aging-related spirometric obstruction, related to lung aging.

pearl: Learn the Age-Friendly 4Ms to up-level your care of chronic lung disease (and any chronic disease for that matter).
episode_title: #408 COPD in Older Adults with Leah Witt, MD & Amber Lanae Martirosov, PharmD

pearl: Supplemental oxygen use may increase the risk for social isolation and loneliness, and has no demonstrated mortality benefit for those with moderate hypoxemia (desaturation only with exertion).
episode_title: #408 COPD in Older Adults with Leah Witt, MD & Amber Lanae Martirosov, PharmD

evidence_links.rationale: LOTT RCT showed no mortality/QoL benefit of oxygen in moderate/exertional hypoxemia.
Suen 2023 found higher social isolation/loneliness among those on supplemental oxygen.

pearl: The cost of inhalers, incorrect inhaler use, side effects of treatment and polypharmacy all affect drug adherence and disease control.
episode_title: #408 COPD in Older Adults with Leah Witt, MD & Amber Lanae Martirosov, PharmD

evidence_links.rationale: Castaldi links cost >$20/month to cost-related non-adherence.
Sanchis shows inhalers frequently used incorrectly, affecting disease control.
Tseng documents high out-of-pocket inhaler costs as barrier to adherence.
Witt 2022 shows polypharmacy burden in older adults with COPD.
Blum on inadequate inspiratory flow relates to incorrect inhaler use reducing efficacy.
Demkowicz explains device patents driving high inhaler prices affecting cost/adherence.
Patel relates to advocacy on drug cost affordability.

pearl: Anterior knee pain is an umbrella term that includes patellofemoral pain
episode_title: #406 Kneedful Things: Knee Pain 201 with Dr. Ted Parks

evidence_links.rationale: Gaitonde covers patellofemoral pain overview but not specifically the umbrella terminology.

pearl: The management of patellofemoral pain consists primarily of physical therapy–surgery should be avoided
episode_title: #406 Kneedful Things: Knee Pain 201 with Dr. Ted Parks

evidence_links.rationale: Gaitonde guideline supports PT-focused management and surgery as last resort for patellofemoral pain.

pearl: Prepatellar bursitis and pes anserine bursitis are common causes of knee pain, and characterized by local tenderness
episode_title: #406 Kneedful Things: Knee Pain 201 with Dr. Ted Parks

pearl: Management of aseptic bursitis includes NSAIDs and stretches, and a cortisone injection can be considered
episode_title: #406 Kneedful Things: Knee Pain 201 with Dr. Ted Parks

evidence_links.rationale: Aaron et al. supports conservative management (rest, NSAIDs, compression) of aseptic bursitis.

pearl: Pain after knee replacement can be divided into pain that persists after surgery and pain that recurs years after surgery
episode_title: #406 Kneedful Things: Knee Pain 201 with Dr. Ted Parks

evidence_links.rationale: Momoli covers exam approach to causes of pain after knee replacement.

pearl: Loosening of hardware can be evaluated by plain film, but a bone scan is more sensitive and should be performed if suspicion is high enough
episode_title: #406 Kneedful Things: Knee Pain 201 with Dr. Ted Parks

evidence_links.rationale: Pinski et al. supports bone scans being very sensitive for identifying loosening.

pearl: Loosening of hardware warrants surgical revision, and diagnosis of septic loosening can be made intra-operatively
episode_title: #406 Kneedful Things: Knee Pain 201 with Dr. Ted Parks

pearl: Diagnostic evaluation for suspected periprosthetic infection often begins with serologic markers of inflammation, with subsequent arthrocentesis for confirmation
episode_title: #406 Kneedful Things: Knee Pain 201 with Dr. Ted Parks

pearl: [Obesity is a chronic disease](https://obesitymedicine.org/what-is-obesity/)
episode_title: #405 Obesity Medicine, GLP1 agonists, Weight Loss Management Tools and Tips

pearl: ! It requires chronic management.
episode_title: #405 Obesity Medicine, GLP1 agonists, Weight Loss Management Tools and Tips

evidence_links.rationale: Weight loss triggers compensatory appetite/metabolic changes, supporting need for chronic management.
Biologically anchored set point explains weight regain, supporting chronic disease management rationale.

pearl: Be sure to mention the diagnosis of obesity to patients because they may read it in their chart.
episode_title: #405 Obesity Medicine, GLP1 agonists, Weight Loss Management Tools and Tips

pearl: Microscopic hematuria with more than 3 RBC per HPF should prompt evaluation
episode_title: #404 Hematuria with Dr. Derek Fine

pearl: Gross hematuria is more suggestive of malignancy than microscopic hematuria
episode_title: #404 Hematuria with Dr. Derek Fine

evidence_links.rationale: Anticoagulated patients' hematuria may reveal cancer earlier; relates to hematuria and malignancy but not gross-vs-micro.

pearl: Risk factors for urothelial malignancy include tobacco use, older age, prior pelvic radiation, and benzene exposure, among others
episode_title: #404 Hematuria with Dr. Derek Fine

evidence_links.rationale: AUA guideline risk stratification identifies older age, smoking, and other urothelial malignancy risk factors.
Aristolochic acid exposure implicated in bladder cancer, a malignancy risk factor exposure item.

pearl: The presence of clots in the urine is more suggestive of a urologic or nonglomerular cause of bleeding
episode_title: #404 Hematuria with Dr. Derek Fine

pearl: Beeturia is more common than you might imagine!
episode_title: #404 Hematuria with Dr. Derek Fine

evidence_links.rationale: Beeturia in up to 14% of population directly supports beeturia commonness.

pearl: For gross hematuria and concern for malignancy, imaging is generally the first step, typically CT urography
episode_title: #404 Hematuria with Dr. Derek Fine

pearl: Hematuria with proteinuria or a rising creatinine should prompt concern for glomerular bleeding and referral to a nephrologist
episode_title: #404 Hematuria with Dr. Derek Fine

pearl: Glomerulonephritis (GN) can broadly be divided into three categories: anti-GBM disease, ANCA GN, and immune complex GN
episode_title: #404 Hematuria with Dr. Derek Fine

evidence_links.rationale: Example of drug-induced ANCA GN, supporting the ANCA GN category.

pearl: A catheter-associated UTI (CAUTI) is defined by the presence of UTI-suggestive symptoms with no other identified source, along with bacteriuria, in a patient with a urinary catheter.
episode_title: #402 Don’t Get Caught with a CAUTI

evidence_links.paper_title: Diagnosis, prevention, and treatment of catheter-associated urinary tract infection in adults: 2009 International Clinical Practice Guidelines from the Infectious Diseases Society of America
evidence_links.rationale: IDSA 2009 guideline defines CAUTI and its diagnostic criteria.

pearl: The presence of cloudy urine or foul odor is NOT a reliable indicator of catheter associated urinary tract infection.

supporting_citations.paper_title: Diagnosis, prevention, and treatment of catheter-associated urinary tract infection in adults: 2009 International Clinical Practice Guidelines from the Infectious Diseases Society of America

episode_title: #402 Don’t Get Caught with a CAUTI

evidence_links.paper_title: Diagnosis, prevention, and treatment of catheter-associated urinary tract infection in adults: 2009 International Clinical Practice Guidelines from the Infectious Diseases Society of America
evidence_links.rationale: Guideline states cloudy/malodorous urine is not suggestive of infection.

pearl: Consider other causes of delirium or acute encephalopathy (such as volume depletion, medications, etc.) before attributing them to CAUTI.
episode_title: #402 Don’t Get Caught with a CAUTI

evidence_links.paper_title: Diagnosis, prevention, and treatment of catheter-associated urinary tract infection in adults: 2009 International Clinical Practice Guidelines from the Infectious Diseases Society of America
evidence_links.rationale: Guideline recommends considering other causes of delirium before attributing to CAUTI.

pearl: Empiric treatment recommended by IDSA is with ceftriaxone or levofloxacin for 7 days for patients who have prompt resolution of signs and symptoms, with extension to 10-14 days if delayed response or if the catheter must stay in place.
episode_title: #402 Don’t Get Caught with a CAUTI

evidence_links.paper_title: Diagnosis, prevention, and treatment of catheter-associated urinary tract infection in adults: 2009 International Clinical Practice Guidelines from the Infectious Diseases Society of America
evidence_links.rationale: Guideline recommends ceftriaxone or levofloxacin for 7 days with prompt resolution.

pearl: Urinary catheters have many adverse effects, and consider alternatives such as straight catheterization when able. Also, remove catheters as soon as indicated.
episode_title: #402 Don’t Get Caught with a CAUTI

pearl: The Mainstreaming Addiction Treatment (MAT) Act has removed the X-waiver requirement for the use of buprenorphine for the treatment of opioid use disorder
episode_title: #401 Updates in Addiction Medicine – SGIM 2023

pearl: The Medication Access and Training Expansion (MATE) Act does outline specific training requirements for new or renewing DEA registrants
episode_title: #401 Updates in Addiction Medicine – SGIM 2023

pearl: Naloxone has been approved for OTC use by the FDA – pricing and timing have yet to be determined
episode_title: #401 Updates in Addiction Medicine – SGIM 2023

pearl: Xylazine has been increasingly implicated in opioid overdoses, as many as 25.8% of opioid overdose deaths in Philadelphia in 2020

supporting_citations.citation_label: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention data

episode_title: #401 Updates in Addiction Medicine – SGIM 2023

evidence_links.rationale: Friedman et al. examined rising xylazine in the opioid supply and its role in overdoses.

pearl: Telemedicine for the provision of medications for opioid use disorder appears to be as effective as reducing overdoses as office-based visits
episode_title: #401 Updates in Addiction Medicine – SGIM 2023

evidence_links.rationale: Jones et al. found telehealth for OUD associated with lower fatal overdose odds.

pearl: Illicit drug use has decreased among adolescents, but overdose deaths have increased in this population

supporting_citations.citation_label: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention data

episode_title: #401 Updates in Addiction Medicine – SGIM 2023

evidence_links.citation_label: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention data
evidence_links.rationale: CDC data showed illicit drug use declined among adolescents while overdose deaths rose.

pearl: A review of electronic health record data showed that we continue to under-screen for alcohol use
episode_title: #401 Updates in Addiction Medicine – SGIM 2023

evidence_links.rationale: Chatterton et al. EHR study found alcohol screening occurred at only 2.6% of visits.
Cited to note AUD medications remain underutilized, related to under-screening.

pearl: A recent study shows promise in the treatment of alcohol use disorder with psilocybin
episode_title: #401 Updates in Addiction Medicine – SGIM 2023

evidence_links.rationale: Bogenschutz RCT showed psilocybin reduced heavy drinking days in AUD.

pearl: Similarly, subanesthetic doses of ketamine were shown to decrease alcohol use in patients with alcohol use disorder
episode_title: #401 Updates in Addiction Medicine – SGIM 2023

evidence_links.rationale: Grabski RCT showed subanesthetic ketamine increased abstinence in severe AUD.
Cited for ketamine's antidepressant effects at subanesthetic doses.

pearl: Artificial intelligence has been used to identify potential medications for the treatment of cocaine use disorder
episode_title: #401 Updates in Addiction Medicine – SGIM 2023

evidence_links.rationale: Gao et al. used AI to identify candidate drugs for cocaine use disorder.

pearl: Monotherapy with a P2Y12 inhibitor (e.g. clopidogrel) might eventually replace aspirin for secondary prevention, especially for patients with high ischemic risk (expert opinion).
segment: Antiplatelet therapy after Acute Coronary Syndrome
clinical_topic: Antiplatelet therapy after Acute Coronary Syndrome
episode_title: #400 Antiplatelets, Anticoagulation for Coronary Artery Disease and Afib

evidence_links.rationale: Zhu 2023 cited specifically for speculation that P2Y12 inhibitors might replace aspirin for secondary prevention in higher ischemic risk patients.

pearl: Individualize the duration of dual and triple therapy for patients with coronary disease, and atrial fibrillation based on ischemic, and bleeding risk.
episode_title: #400 Antiplatelets, Anticoagulation for Coronary Artery Disease and Afib

evidence_links.rationale: Abadie 2020 provides the pathway for individualized antithrombotic management by ischemic and bleeding risk.
Lawton 2022 gives recommendations on triple/dual therapy duration and longer DAPT in higher-risk patients.
Knuuti 2019 guides duration/monotherapy decisions in stable IHD with new afib.
Kirchof 2016 supports individualized dual vs monotherapy based on ischemic/bleeding risk.

pearl: Monotherapy with an oral anticoagulant can be used for patients with stable ischemic heart disease who develop atrial fibrillation (afib) —
episode_title: #400 Antiplatelets, Anticoagulation for Coronary Artery Disease and Afib

evidence_links.rationale: Knuuti 2019 supports OAC monotherapy for stable IHD patients 6-12 months from revascularization who develop afib.
Kirchof 2016 supports OAC monotherapy after prior CABG in atrial fibrillation.
Yasuda 2019 supports OAC monotherapy after prior CABG in afib.

pearl: The American College of Rheumatology (ACR) 2010 guidelines for RA are a helpful starting point (
episode_title: #399 Wisely Ordering Autoantibodies – ACP IM2023

evidence_links.rationale: Aletaha 2010 is the ACR 2010 RA guideline described as a helpful starting point.

pearl: Every patient who presents to the emergency department with ascites should undergo diagnostic paracentesis.
episode_title: # 398:Live! From SHM: Inpatient Cirrhosis Management

pearl: If a patient is not improving on empiric treatment of SBP within the first 48 hours, a repeat diagnostic paracentesis may be indicated.
episode_title: # 398:Live! From SHM: Inpatient Cirrhosis Management

evidence_links.rationale: Biggins guideline covers SBP definition and treatment, relevant to repeat paracentesis for non-improvement.

pearl: There are 3 evidence-based indications for use of albumin in patients with cirrhosis: prevention of paracentesis induced circulatory dysfunction, treatment of SBP (in combination with antibiotics) and diagnosis/treatment of AKI/HRS.
episode_title: # 398:Live! From SHM: Inpatient Cirrhosis Management

evidence_links.rationale: Biggins guideline supports albumin use in SBP and HRS-AKI management.

pearl: Treatment of acute hepatic encephalopathy should involve increasing the patient’s home lactulose regimen.
episode_title: # 398:Live! From SHM: Inpatient Cirrhosis Management

evidence_links.rationale: Vilstrup guideline directly supports lactulose as mainstay treatment for hepatic encephalopathy.

pearl: A palliative approach to patients with cirrhosis can and should be employed at any stage of disease.
episode_title: # 398:Live! From SHM: Inpatient Cirrhosis Management

evidence_links.rationale: Rogal cited to support palliative care at any stage of cirrhosis.

pearl: To distinguish globus sensation from dysphagia, ask about how the symptom changes while swallowing. Globus will typically improve while swallowing and dysphagia will not change or worsen.
episode_title: #395 Dysphagia with Dr. Diana Snyder

pearl: Oropharyngeal dysphagia includes any etiology of dysphagia resulting from pathology above the upper esophageal sphincter.
episode_title: #395 Dysphagia with Dr. Diana Snyder

pearl: Symptoms of oropharyngeal dysphagia include coughing, nasal regurgitation of liquids, and halitosis.
episode_title: #395 Dysphagia with Dr. Diana Snyder

evidence_links.rationale: Liu 2018 references oropharyngeal symptoms like coughing, nasal regurgitation, halitosis.

pearl: Esophageal dysphagia includes any etiology of dysphagia resulting from pathology below the upper esophageal sphincter.
episode_title: #395 Dysphagia with Dr. Diana Snyder

pearl: A modified barium swallow study is useful in the evaluation of patients with symptoms of oropharyngeal dysphagia.
episode_title: #395 Dysphagia with Dr. Diana Snyder

evidence_links.rationale: Liu 2018 supports modified barium swallow for suspected oropharyngeal dysphagia.

pearl: Studies for motility disorders including manometry should be considered in patients with dysphagia to liquids.
episode_title: #395 Dysphagia with Dr. Diana Snyder

pearl: Eosinophilic esophagitis is an increasingly prevalent etiology of dysphagia.
episode_title: #395 Dysphagia with Dr. Diana Snyder

evidence_links.rationale: Dellon 2018 documents increasing EoE prevalence.

pearl: The diagnosis of eosinophilic esophagitis is made with 1)clinical symptoms including dysphagia and 2) histologic evidence of >15 eosinophils per high powered field.
episode_title: #395 Dysphagia with Dr. Diana Snyder

evidence_links.rationale: Hirano 2020 uses the >15 eosinophils/hpf histologic threshold, though for treatment response.

pearl: Xylazine (aka tranq) is an alpha-2 adrenergic agonist commonly found as an adulterant of fentanyl
episode_title: #392 Live! Opioid and Xylazine Withdrawal in the Hospital

evidence_links.rationale: Ehrman-Dupre describes xylazine as a central and peripheral alpha-2 adrenergic agonist similar to clonidine.

pearl: Recognize xylazine withdrawal in the patient on an adequate regimen for opioid withdrawal who has residual anxiety/restlessness
episode_title: #392 Live! Opioid and Xylazine Withdrawal in the Hospital

pearl: Recognize xylazine withdrawal in the patient with an overdose who does not respond to naloxone
episode_title: #392 Live! Opioid and Xylazine Withdrawal in the Hospital

evidence_links.rationale: Ball cited to support recognizing xylazine in overdose not responding to naloxone.

pearl: Xylazine appears to cause necrotic skin wounds at sites of frequent injection
episode_title: #392 Live! Opioid and Xylazine Withdrawal in the Hospital

evidence_links.rationale: Ehrman-Dupre describes possible xylazine wound mechanisms.

pearl: As needed benzodiazepines (e.g. clonazepam) along with adjunctive clonidine, tizanidine, gabapentin, or antipsychotics are commonly deployed in the management of xylazine withdrawal
episode_title: #392 Live! Opioid and Xylazine Withdrawal in the Hospital

evidence_links.rationale: Ehrman-Dupre attributes ICU/ward management options for xylazine withdrawal.

pearl: Weaning patients off full agonist opioids prior to hospital discharge is dangerous because it lowers opioid tolerance and can put them at risk for overdose if they return to illicit opioid use.
episode_title: #392 Live! Opioid and Xylazine Withdrawal in the Hospital

evidence_links.rationale: Lyden shows increased overdose risk in the early post-discharge period, supporting danger of weaning.

pearl: When taking a history from a patient with a venous thrombotic event, ascertain their personal history of thrombosis, family history of thrombosis, and potential provoking factors for the current episode, including surgery, travel, chronic inflammatory diseases, hospitalization, estrogen exposure, and obesity.
segment: History-taking in a patient with a venous thromboembolism (VTE)
clinical_topic: History-taking in a patient with a venous thromboembolism (VTE)
episode_title: #391 Hypercoagulable Work-Up with Dr. Jean Connors

evidence_links.rationale: Heit et al supports pregnancy/postpartum as a provoking factor to assess in VTE history.
Abdul Sultan supports postpartum thrombotic risk as a provoking factor.
Pomp et al supports obesity as a VTE provoking factor to ascertain.
Stegeman supports estrogen/OCP exposure as a provoking factor (OR 3.5).

pearl: When considering testing for thrombophilia, ask yourself: why are you testing, and what are you going to do with the results?
segment: History-taking in a patient with a venous thromboembolism (VTE)
clinical_topic: History-taking in a patient with a venous thromboembolism (VTE)
episode_title: #391 Hypercoagulable Work-Up with Dr. Jean Connors

pearl: A patient’s thrombophilia status will generally not change their personal anticoagulation management (with the exception of antiphospholipid syndrome.) However, implications of thrombophilia status for female family members considering OCP initiation or pregnancy may be a motivation for testing.
segment: History-taking in a patient with a venous thromboembolism (VTE)
clinical_topic: History-taking in a patient with a venous thromboembolism (VTE)
episode_title: #391 Hypercoagulable Work-Up with Dr. Jean Connors

evidence_links.rationale: Connors 2017 states results usually don't change anticoagulation but matter for family planning.
Stegeman OCP thrombosis risk informs counseling of female family members considering OCPs.

pearl: The five core tests in an inherited thrombophilia panel are: prothrombin gene mutation, factor V leiden, protein C, protein S, and antithrombin. Thrombophilia testing should generally not be performed in the acute setting or while the patient is on anticoagulation, due to the impact of acute inflammation and anticoagulants on certain test results.
segment: History-taking in a patient with a venous thromboembolism (VTE)
clinical_topic: History-taking in a patient with a venous thromboembolism (VTE)
episode_title: #391 Hypercoagulable Work-Up with Dr. Jean Connors

evidence_links.rationale: Bezemer notes negative standard panel doesn't eliminate risk, relevant to interpreting core tests.

pearl: Antiphospholipid syndrome is diagnosed with the revised Sapporo criteria, which incorporates clinical and laboratory parameters. Diagnosing antiphospholipid syndrome is important for management, as it is treated with warfarin rather than DOACs.
segment: History-taking in a patient with a venous thromboembolism (VTE)
clinical_topic: History-taking in a patient with a venous thromboembolism (VTE)
episode_title: #391 Hypercoagulable Work-Up with Dr. Jean Connors

evidence_links.rationale: Miyakis defines the revised Sapporo criteria for APS diagnosis.
TRAPs trial supports warfarin over rivaroxaban for thrombotic APS.

pearl: Hypertension is a systolic blood pressure (BP) ≥ 130mm Hg and/or a diastolic BP ≥ 80 mmHg (
episode_title: #390 Resistant Hypertension

evidence_links.paper_title: 2017 ACC/AHA Guideline for the Prevention, Detection, Evaluation, and Management of High Blood Pressure in Adults

pearl: The two major questions when evaluating an adrenal incidentaloma are whether the mass is malignant and whether it is functioning (producing hormones.)
episode_title: #377 Adrenal Incidentalomas with Dr. William Young

pearl: A non-contrast CT scan allows assessment of the density of the adrenal mass, which provides information about whether the mass is lipid-rich (<10 Hounsfield units) or lipid-poor (>10 Hounsfield units.) Lipid-rich masses are most likely benign adenomas and are highly unlikely to be cancer or pheochromocytomas, while lipid-poor masses may be a primary adrenal malignancy, metastatic cancer, pheochromocytoma, or a lipid-poor benign adenoma.
episode_title: #377 Adrenal Incidentalomas with Dr. William Young

evidence_links.rationale: Young 2007 supports non-contrast CT attenuation distinguishing lipid-rich from lipid-poor masses.
Corwin 2022 discusses limited utility of contrast wash-out, informing which imaging features matter.

pearl: Dr. Young’s expert opinion is that all patients with a lipid-rich mass should receive one follow-up scan at 1 year. Patients with clearly lipid-poor masses (e.g. unenhanced CT attenuation >20 Hounsfield units) should undergo surgical intervention or close monitoring with indefinite follow-up.
episode_title: #377 Adrenal Incidentalomas with Dr. William Young

evidence_links.rationale: Iniguez-Ariza 2017 supports that imaging phenotype over size guides surgical decisions.

pearl: All patients with adrenal incidentalomas should be screened for subclinical Cushing’s syndrome with ACTH, DHEAS, and cortisol levels. If DHEAS is >100 mcg/dL, this suggests that the patient does not have subclinical Cushing’s and the evaluation can stop, while if DHEAS is <100 mcg/dL, the evaluation should proceed with a 1 mg overnight dexamethasone suppression test. Dr. Young also recommends that all patients with a positive 1 mg overnight dexamethasone suppression test undergo a confirmatory 8 mg overnight dexamethasone suppression test prior to surgical intervention, to avoid false positives.
episode_title: #377 Adrenal Incidentalomas with Dr. William Young

evidence_links.rationale: Carafone 2021 supports DHEAS use in evaluating subclinical Cushing's.
Dennedy 2017 supports DHEAS as biomarker to decide whether further Cushing's testing is needed.
Chiodini 2009 provides rationale for why evaluating subclinical Cushing's matters.
Kebebew 2021 describes subclinical Cushing's clinical features.

pearl: Patients with adrenal incidentalomas with an unenhanced CT attenuation  >10 Hounsfield units should undergo screening for pheochromocytoma with plasma fractionated metanephrines.
episode_title: #377 Adrenal Incidentalomas with Dr. William Young

evidence_links.rationale: Canu 2019 supports pheochromocytoma screening when attenuation >10 HU and not for lipid-rich.
Gruber 2019 shows many pheochromocytomas found incidentally, supporting screening rationale.

pearl: Patients with adrenal incidentalomas who are hypertensive or hypokalemic should undergo screening for primary hyperaldosteronism with morning aldosterone and renin levels.
episode_title: #377 Adrenal Incidentalomas with Dr. William Young

evidence_links.rationale: Fassnacht 2016 guideline supports aldosteronism screening in hypertensive/hypokalemic patients with incidentalomas.
Funder 2016 guides aldosterone/renin screening for primary hyperaldosteronism.

pearl: Delirium is a clinical syndrome and it is NOT just dichotomous (either present or absent). The severity and duration of delirium matter for long-term clinical outcomes.
episode_title: #375 Delirium in the Hospital featuring Dr. Esther Oh

evidence_links.rationale: DECIDE study shows delirium severity associated with worse cognitive outcomes, supporting that severity/duration matter.

pearl: Delirium is associated with ongoing brain inflammation and neuronal damage, and increases rates of dementia, functional decline, and institutionalization.
episode_title: #375 Delirium in the Hospital featuring Dr. Esther Oh

evidence_links.rationale: Casey 2020 links delirium to NfL biomarkers of neuronal damage.
DECIDE shows delirium increases risk of future cognitive decline and dementia.

pearl: Up to 30-40% of delirium can be prevented with multicomponent prevention strategies. Behavioral strategies may help reduce severity and duration even when delirium is present.
episode_title: #375 Delirium in the Hospital featuring Dr. Esther Oh

evidence_links.rationale: Inouye 1999 cited for the 30-40% preventable figure.
Marcantonio 2001 cited for the 30-40% preventable figure.
Oh 2018 describes multicomponent prevention bundles.

pearl: There are several tools to help identify delirium, including the CAM, 4AT, and the ultra-brief CAM, but ultimately it is a clinical diagnosis.
episode_title: #375 Delirium in the Hospital featuring Dr. Esther Oh

evidence_links.rationale: Inouye 1990 is the source of the CAM tool.

pearl: The etiology of delirium is usually multifactorial and requires a thoughtful clinical evaluation.  Don’t just check the UA until it becomes abnormal.
episode_title: #375 Delirium in the Hospital featuring Dr. Esther Oh

pearl: There is no evidence that antipsychotics help to prevent or treat delirium.  In general, avoid antipsychotics unless patient or staff safety is at risk.
episode_title: #375 Delirium in the Hospital featuring Dr. Esther Oh

evidence_links.rationale: Nikooie 2019 finds no evidence supporting antipsychotics for treatment of delirium.
Oh 2019 finds no evidence supporting antipsychotics for prevention of delirium.

pearl: ADHD is a childhood illness that can persist into adulthood.  However, it may go unrecognized until it is made more obvious by increased cognitive demands.
episode_title: #374 ADHD with Dr. Kevin Simon

evidence_links.rationale: Thapar and Cooper support ADHD symptoms present in childhood across settings.

pearl: ADHD is a disease of executive function, and is characterized by inattention, hyperactivity and impulsivity, or both.
episode_title: #374 ADHD with Dr. Kevin Simon

evidence_links.rationale: Volkow and Swanson describe ADHD as impaired executive function with symptom subtypes.

pearl: Stimulants are first line medications for ADHD.  Dr. Simon favors methylphenidate, and long acting formulations are preferred in general.
episode_title: #374 ADHD with Dr. Kevin Simon

evidence_links.rationale: Medical Letter supports stimulants as first-line, long-acting preferred.

pearl: Non-stimulant medications for ADHD include atomoxetine, guanfacine, clonidine, and viloxazine.  Viloxazine has been shown to improve concentration, similar to stimulant therapy.
episode_title: #374 ADHD with Dr. Kevin Simon

evidence_links.rationale: Nasser et al. shows viloxazine improves concentration similar to stimulants.
Newcorn et al. supports guanfacine and clonidine ER for inattention.

pearl: Comorbid substance use disorder is not a contraindication for stimulant treatment for ADHD.  Appropriate treatment for ADHD can actually improve retention in care.

episode_title: #374 ADHD with Dr. Kevin Simon

evidence_links.rationale: Kast et al. supports SUD not being a contraindication and improved retention in care.

pearl: Adrenal crisis = hypoadrenalism (adrenal insufficiency) that becomes so severe that there is resulting organ failure (eg, nausea/vomiting, hypotension).
episode_title: #372 Endocrine Emergencies featuring Dr. Sara Markley Webster

evidence_links.rationale: Rushworth 2019 covers causes and mortality of adrenal crisis, informing the definition.

pearl: Stress dose steroid dosing: ≥ triple home dose, or 100 mg IV hydrocortisone once followed by 50mg every 6 hours. Note: ≥50mg hydrocortisone/day covers both glucocorticoid and mineralocorticoid receptors.
episode_title: #372 Endocrine Emergencies featuring Dr. Sara Markley Webster

evidence_links.rationale: Bornstein 2016 provides the specific hydrocortisone stress-dose regimen cited.
Puar 2016 supports that ≥50mg hydrocortisone covers both glucocorticoid and mineralocorticoid receptors.

pearl: Myxedema coma = hypothyroidism that becomes so severe that there is resulting end organ damage (eg, altered mental status-look for characteristic finding of slurred speech).
episode_title: #372 Endocrine Emergencies featuring Dr. Sara Markley Webster

evidence_links.rationale: Ylli 2019 describes clinical features of myxedema coma including altered mental status and high mortality.

pearl: Thyroid hormone replacement dosing: IV levothyroxine 200-400 micrograms loading dose, then 75% of 1.6mcg/kg/day IV. Note: IV is preferred to PO dosing due to the risk of patients having gut edema and not having adequate PO absorption.
episode_title: #372 Endocrine Emergencies featuring Dr. Sara Markley Webster

evidence_links.rationale: Jonklaas 2014 gives IV levothyroxine dosing and preference over PO due to absorption.

pearl: Thyroid storm = hyperthyroidism that becomes so severe that there is resulting end organ damage (eg, heart failure).
episode_title: #372 Endocrine Emergencies featuring Dr. Sara Markley Webster

evidence_links.rationale: Devereaux 2016 supports high mortality of untreated thyroid storm, underscoring severity.

pearl: Medical management of thyroid storm: shut off thyroid hormone production with methimazole or propylthiouracil, followed by iodine. Add beta blocker to control heart rate. Note: propranolol will also inhibit peripheral T4 to T3 conversion.
segment: ● Thyroid storm identification, workup and management
clinical_topic: ● Thyroid storm identification, workup and management
episode_title: #372 Endocrine Emergencies featuring Dr. Sara Markley Webster

evidence_links.rationale: Ross 2016 notes steroid inhibition of T4-to-T3 conversion relevant to thyroid storm management.

pearl: If no improvement of thyroid storm within 24-48 hours, will require thyroidectomy or plasmapheresis–involve consultants early.
episode_title: #372 Endocrine Emergencies featuring Dr. Sara Markley Webster

evidence_links.rationale: Chiha 2013 supports thyroidectomy/plasmapheresis and early consultation in refractory thyroid storm.

pearl: Add high dose hydrocortisone when concerned for myxedema coma (stress dosing) or thyroid storm (300mg load followed by 100mg IV q8 hours) due to risk of concomitant adrenal insufficiency.
episode_title: #372 Endocrine Emergencies featuring Dr. Sara Markley Webster

evidence_links.rationale: Ross 2016 supports high-dose glucocorticoids for concomitant adrenal insufficiency and T4-to-T3 inhibition.

pearl: Advantages to regional anesthesia over general anesthesia can include less postoperative nausea and vomiting (PONV), less post-anesthetic “hangover”, faster awakening, and fewer postoperative narcotic needs.

pearl: Currently there is still NO robust evidence to prove regional anesthesia lowers the risk of postop delirium or cognitive dysfunction over general anesthesia.
episode_title: #365: Perioperative Medicine with Anesthesiologist Dr. Angela Selzer

evidence_links.rationale: REGAIN showed spinal was not superior to general anesthesia for recovery outcomes, supporting lack of robust benefit.

pearl: Contraindications to regional anesthesia include insufficient anticoagulation hold times, coagulopathy, infection at the injection site, or a need to monitor neuromuscular function postop.
